mechanism: Each landing zone gets a user-assigned managed identity with Owner at subscription scope, OIDC federated credentials scoped to org/repo/env, and Directory Reader Graph permissions.
why: Without a single identity, each capability (workflows, jobs, Graph queries) needs a separate identity with its own RBAC and OIDC config — permission surface multiplies with every new capability.

related.note: Jobs authenticate as the landing zone identity — no separate identity per job.
OIDC federation on this identity lets GitHub Actions authenticate without secrets.
Directory Reader allows automation jobs to query Entra ID.
Profile provides owner and engineer contacts consumed by the identity for tag values and incident routing.

violations: Managed identity shared across multiple landing zones.
Landing zone identity with Owner at management group scope instead of subscription.
OIDC federated credentials not scoped to a specific environment.
